identity.title: Red Flags From The Beginning
identity.styleSentence: A high-energy (143 BPM) pop-rock anthem driven by a driving F-major key, featuring distorted guitar swells and modern drill-style hi-hats layered over deep 808 bass pulses to create an urgent yet emotionally raw soundscape.
identity.vocalStyle: Dynamic duet with spoken-word intros and verses alternating between intimate close-mic female leads and gritty male replies, culminating in a soaring but weary chorus.

lyrics.text:
[Intro - Female Spoken, Close Mic]
Fucked [Intro]

[spoken male vocals]
Fuckup ed up from the from the beginnbeginning
ing...
Always was...
Always was
Always will be...

Always will be[Male Spoken, Low]
We should’ve seen it.

[Instrumental Break]

[distorted guitar swell, drill hats, 808 pulse]

[Verse 1 - Female Lead]
Fucked up from the jump, we were smiling through the sirens
Red flags on the walls, but we called it perfect timi[Male Spoken]
Always ng
I kept looking for the green, like a sign I could believe in
But the only green was money when the bills came screaming
You left socks on the floor like a war I couldn’t win
Unfinished water by the sink, same fight again
Laundry in a mountain, folding turned to blame
Overtime kept calling and we answered it by name

[Male Reply]
I was tired, you were distant
We were loud, never listening

[Female Lead]
You got secret with your phone, I got colder with my tone
Two people in a house still feeling alone

[Pre-Chorus - Female]
Eyes bloodshot
Not from the rot
But from reading every silence like a warning shot

[Male]
Stay in your lane

[Female]
But the lane was a graveyard
Every marker had our name
Every name had a scar

w[Chorus - Female Lead + Male Echo]
Maybe I as...

should stop
[Female Whisper]
AlMaybe I should drop
Let the mountain keep the weight I never asked to carry up
Maybe I should fall
Echo down the hall
Let the silence be the answer when I can’t stand up at all
Stop, drop
Watch it all dissolve
Stop, drop
Nothing left to solve

[Instrumental Break]

[guitar riff, 808 slide, snare build]

[Verse 2 - Female Lead]
We fought about the dishes, fought about the rent
Fought about the words we never really meant
You said I was dramatic, I said you never cared
Truth is we were both just swinging at the air

[Male Reply]
You checked out first

[Female Reply]
You locked up worse

[Male]
You wanted proof

[Female]
You hid the hurt

[Female Lead]
Had the green light gleaming, but it never meant go
Just another little lie in a pretty red glow
I should’ve seen the signs when love felt like a debt
When every kiss came loaded with a thing we won’t forget

[Pre-Chorus - Female + Male]
Eyes bloodshot
Not from the rot
Not from the drinks
Not from the smoke
But from the mirror showing both of us the joke

[Male]
We called it passion

[Female]
It was damage in disguise

[Both]
We kept feeding the fire
Then acted surprised

[Chorus - Female Lead + Male Echo]
Maybe I should stop
Maybe I should drop
Let the mountain keep the weight I never asked to carry up
Maybe I should fall
Echo down the hall
Let the silence be the answer when I can’t stand up at all
Stop, drop
Watch it all dissolve
wStop, drop
Nothing left to solve

[Bridge - Female, Vulnerable]
Hate to admit it
But God, I’m terrified
Tarred by my own hands
Feathered by my pride

[Male, Soft]
I loved you wrong

[Female]
I stayed too long

[Both]
We were doomed before the song

[Solo Full Band Instrumental Break]

[explosive guitar solo, crash cymbals, 808 undercurrent]

[Final Pre-Chorus - Female, Rising]
Eyes bloodshot
Not from the rot
Not from the mountain
Not from the cold
But from all those little warnings
That we watched and still ignored

[pause break]

[Final Chorus - Female Lead, Full Band]
Maybe I should stop
Maybe I should drop
Let the mountain keep the weight I never asked to carry up
aMaybe yI s should fall
will be...

[Ending Instrumental Break]

[clean guitar fading into distorted low 808]Echo down the hall
Let the silence be the answer when I can’t stand up at all
But the bloodshot fades
And the red flags wave
And the truth comes late
But it comes anyway

[Post-Chorus - Female + Male]
Maybe I should stop
Maybe I should drop
Maybe I already have

[Outro - Female Spoken]
Fucked up from the beginning...

[Male Spoken]
Always was...

[Female Whisper]
Always will be...

[Ending Instrumental Break]

[clean guitar fading into distorted low 808]

analysis.summary: The song depicts a toxic relationship doomed from the start, characterized by domestic chaos, emotional neglect, and the painful realization that love was actually a burden disguised as passion.

keywords.imageryPrompt: A cracked red wall covered in peeling paint, casting long ominous shadows against a dark background.
Blue emergency lights flashing rapidly through rain-streaked windows reflecting on wet asphalt streets.
Stacks of green dollar bills glowing under a harsh spotlight while flowers wither in the background.
A vast empty hallway stretching endlessly into darkness where dust motes hang motionless in still air.
Orange flames consuming a wooden structure, illuminating tear-streaked faces with intense light and smoke.
A pale white moon hanging high in a starless night sky above a lone silhouette standing on a cliff edge.
